author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: GH-613: public SDK bulk item-mutation helper on commitWorkspaceTransaction — atomic create/update/close batches without hand-rolled step+compensation wiring

public SDK exposes a bulk item-mutation transaction helper (create/update/close batch) built on commitWorkspaceTransaction with default inspect (exists-by-id/provenance key) and idempotent compensation (create→close, update→restore captured fields); stable transactionId makes batches resumable; failure-injection tests cover partial batches; SDK docs show the wrapper replacing a hand-rolled step array; GH-613 is resolved

message: Filed from GH-613: commitWorkspaceTransaction (pm-4e12) shipped low-level; the ubiquitous N-item batch case forces every sync package (pm-csv/pm-github/pm-jira/pm-linear) to reinvent step/compensation wiring. One-call correct-by-construction bulk helper.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implemented: public commitItemMutations in NEW src/sdk/item-transaction.ts (exported via sdk barrel), wrapping commitWorkspaceTransaction with correct-by-construction wiring per op: create = explicit stable id AS idempotency key (exists-by-id inspect; compensation close-with-reason by default or delete via createCompensation:"delete", both guarded idempotent); update = durable history-marker applied-detection ("bulk-item-transaction <txid> <stepId> apply/compensate" messages — field comparison is a moving target and an always-pending inspect would make compensateAppliedSteps SKIP the restore, found via failing test) + version-restore compensation from captured pre-mutation updated_at; close = already-terminal target adopted as applied (never re-closes), version-restore compensation. Results keyed by derived step id {index}-{op}-{target}. 6 specs: atomic commit+replay, full reverse compensation (create closed/update restored/close reopened), delete-compensation, crash-interrupt + resume without duplication (WorkspaceTransactionInterruptedError), pre-closed adoption, input validation. SDK.md example added showing the wrapper replacing a hand-rolled step array. Manual temp-dir verify via dist: committed batch + rollback semantics correct.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Transaction-step design learning: commitWorkspaceTransaction's compensateAppliedSteps gates each rollback on inspect() reporting "applied" (started && applied). A step whose inspect always returns "pending" (tempting for naturally-idempotent updates) silently SKIPS its own compensation — the batch reports rolled-back while the update persists. Any step type whose applied-ness is not derivable from domain state needs a durable discriminator; item history messages are the right substrate (immutable, replay-safe, author-attributed). Marker pairs (apply/compensate, scan from newest) also make post-compensation retries re-apply correctly where a single marker would wedge the step as permanently "applied".

value.note: bulk transaction API contract and example

patch.value: Expose commitItemMutations as the public atomic item-batch SDK primitive over durable workspace transactions.
Package authors can commit resumable create/update/close batches without hand-writing transaction steps and compensations.
Public SDK supports stable replay, crash resume, reverse compensation, close/delete create policies, authoritative authors, and defensive runtime validation.
Implemented and verified in PR #616 at exact head bafb9b53; local and GitHub gates green, all review feedback addressed.
